detail:
GENUINE config/env-stranding gap. The core defect holds; one tangential side-claim by the reviewer is wrong.

Core defect: when OpenClaw reports a BYO billing/auth failure, RuntimeBYOErrorReportView.post (apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:2167-2230) calls ONLY mark_credential_error. It never calls tenant.bump_pending_config(), regenerate_tenant_config(), or apply_byo_credentials_to_container(). This is asymmetric with the paste path (apps/byo_models/views.py:136-153) and the delete path (views.py:178-193), both of which deliberately do all three so the live container openclaw.json and env stay consistent with cred state.

At paste time the container is forced into a BYO-only state: openclaw.json pins the Claude primary with an INTENTIONALLY EMPTY fallback list (config_generator.py:1064-1067 -- the empty fallback is by design so a billing failure surfaces instead of dropping to a metered model) and apply_byo_credentials_to_container (azure_client.py:1176-1184) strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and binds CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN. After the error report flips the cred to ERROR, the SOURCE state would self-heal IF regenerated: _byo_model_extras (config_generator.py:999-1006) and apply_byo_credentials_to_container (azure_client.py:1143-1150) both exclude ERROR creds, so a regen would repopulate metered fallbacks AND restore ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. But nothing on the error path triggers that regen, so the running container keeps the stale openclaw.json and stale env. The hourly apply-pending-configs cron skips this tenant (config_version >= pending_config_version, tasks.py:171; the error path never bumped pending). The runtime entrypoint only reconciles at boot. No dedicated BYO error-reconcile cron exists -- the only apps/cron BYO endpoints (rollout-byo-image-bump / rollout-byo-persona-refresh, cron/views.py:1561+) are manual workflow_dispatch rollouts that do not touch a failed cred.

User impact: after a user's Claude subscription hits a billing/auth failure, their assistant goes dark for ALL turns, not just the failing one. The primary model cannot be called, there is no fallback to a platform model, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY was stripped from the container. Recovery is only incidental (any unrelated config bump, image rollout, or the user re-pasting/disconnecting the cred triggers a regen that self-heals) -- there is no automatic recovery on the failure itself.

CORRECTION to the reviewer's secondary claim: the reviewer asserts mark_credential_error returns None today because of a PENDING-exclusion bug, so even the cred-status flip does not happen. That is WRONG. A live, working BYO cred is in VERIFIED status (paste leaves it PENDING at services.py:136, but it becomes VERIFIED once verified; the test at apps/integrations/tests.py:138-144 seeds VERIFIED and asserts the flip to ERROR succeeds at lines 170-173). mark_credential_error excludes only PENDING (services.py:186-190), so a VERIFIED cred IS matched and DOES flip to ERROR, and the rose error banner DOES surface. So the user IS told to reconnect/disconnect (which is exactly what triggers self-heal). The cred-status/banner side of the loop works; only the live container config/env reconcile is missing. Severity stays high because the assistant is fully unresponsive until the user acts on that banner.
suggested_fix: On the actionable-failure branch of RuntimeBYOErrorReportView.post (after mark_credential_error returns a non-None cred), mirror the paste/delete coupling: call tenant.bump_pending_config(), regenerate_tenant_config(tenant), and apply_byo_credentials_to_container(tenant), each wrapped in try/except so a failure is logged but still returns 200 (the runtime should not retry). Because _byo_model_extras and apply_byo_credentials_to_container both exclude ERROR creds, this regenerates openclaw.json with a working metered primary + real fallbacks and restores ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the container env, so the assistant keeps responding on a platform model while the user reconnects. Optionally add a lightweight periodic BYO-reconcile cron as a backstop for tenants whose error-path regen failed, since the apply-pending-configs cron will not pick them up without a pending bump.
evidence: apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:2205-2230 -- error path calls only mark_credential_error; no bump_pending_config/regenerate/apply_byo_credentials_to_container
apps/byo_models/views.py:136-153 and 178-193 -- paste and delete paths DO call all three; error path is asymmetric
apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:1064-1067 -- BYO primary gets an intentionally empty fallback list
apps/orchestrator/azure_client.py:1176-1184 -- paste strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and binds CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; only restored when apply runs with no/error cred (lines 1143-1150 exclude ERROR)
apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:999-1006 -- _byo_model_extras excludes ERROR creds, so a regen after error would restore metered fallbacks
apps/orchestrator/tasks.py:171 -- apply task bails when config_version >= pending_config_version; error path never bumps pending
apps/cron/views.py:1561+ and apps/cron/urls.py:44-51 -- only manual rollout-byo endpoints; no BYO error-reconcile cron
apps/integrations/tests.py:138-173 -- cred seeded VERIFIED and asserted to flip to ERROR, disproving the reviewer's 'mark_credential_error returns None today' side-claim
